game_name: The Simpsons: Bart vs. the Space Mutants

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Reveals disguised Space Mutants and possessed characters; enables progression through all levels
Primary objective; destroyed via jumping on mutants or prank calls (e.g., spray-painting Moe's apron)
Collected by defeating aliens; used to purchase rockets and cherry bombs
Purchased with coins; destroys objects at distance; can hit Kwik-E-Mart sign to unlock sound test
Purchased with coins; alternative destruction method
Collected upon defeating disguised mutants; spelling family names unlocks aid in boss battles
Primary objective; obtained from jumping on trash cans or defeating miniboss while wearing X-Ray Specs
Lucky Roulette and Dizzy Doors puzzles; yield items or unlock progression
Primary objective in amusement park setting
Limited usability; used for specific sections only

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Level 4 - Springfield Museum of Natural History
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Primary objective in museum with tar pit and dinosaur bones
Final objective; highest difficulty context; no boss battle recorded after collection
Present at end of first four levels; can be aided by summoned family members

typical_relief_triggers: Successfully identifying and jumping on disguised mutants
Summoning family members to assist in boss battles
Collecting letters to spell family names
typical_tension_triggers: Encountering disguised mutants camouflaged as NPCs
Running out of coins before purchasing necessary equipment
Boss encounters without family member assistance
Traversing dangerous areas (tar pit, nuclear plant)
Timed minigame sections (Dizzy Doors with timer)
Loss of life accompanied by 'Eat my shorts!' voice line
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Gradual difficulty escalation across five levels; early levels introduce collection mechanics and equipment systems; mid-game introduces minigames and spatial complexity; late-game emphasizes high-difficulty object collection in hazardous environments; boss battles punctuate level completion

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Trash cans in Springfield Mall jumpable for hidden items; background gaps and elevated platforms conceal developer initials and mystery items
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden developer credits disguised as collectible initials (RK, LK/AK, HW, AW, KMB, PJB, KAK); activated through specific sequences (jumping patterns, door orders, button combinations) or X-Ray Specs activation

difficulty_curve.early_game: Streets of Springfield: Introduction to X-Ray Specs, basic mutant jumping, coin collection, and equipment purchasing; straightforward navigation with initial skateboard section
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Springfield Mall and Krustyland: Introduction of minigames (Lucky Roulette, Dizzy Doors), expanded level complexity, increased mutant density, trash can farming mechanics requiring vertical platforming
difficulty_curve.late_game: Museum of Natural History and Nuclear Power Plant: High-difficulty object collection in hazardous terrain (tar pit with dinosaur bones, nuclear facility); sustained enemy pressure; final boss without family assist option documented
notable_spikes: Level 2 (Springfield Mall): Trash can hex-value puzzle (101011 binary sequence) requires precise directional input
Level 3 (Krustyland): Dizzy Doors timed puzzle with nine-door sequence (1-2-1-1-9-5-2)
Level 4 (Museum): Navigation through tar pit environment with multiple jumping requirements
Level 5 (Nuclear Power Plant): Final gauntlet without documented boss assist mechanic

notable_level_patterns.description: Each level assigns unique collectible type: Level 1 = purple objects, Level 2 = hats, Level 3 = balloons, Level 4 = exit signs, Level 5 = nuclear rods. Objective count varies per level. Mechanic ties thematic content to level setting.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Level 1 (Streets of Springfield) - Spray-paint Moe's purple apron via prank call

notable_level_patterns.description: Boss battles in Levels 1-4 gain advantage from collecting family member initials (spelling MARGE, HOMER, LISA, MAGGIE) by defeating disguised mutants. Collecting 1+ letter summons that family member to aid in subsequent boss encounter.

Progression requires purchasing rockets or cherry bombs with coins farmed from mutant defeats. Equipment unlocks access to sealed areas and special objectives (e.g., Kwik-E-Mart sign destruction unlocks sound test).
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Level 1 (Streets of Springfield) - Rocket purchase enables sign destruction

notable_level_patterns.description: Mid-to-late game introduces minigames with strict sequencing requirements. Dizzy Doors requires 7-door sequence completion within time limit; Lucky Roulette requires 5-number sequence (5-4-3-8-6) with button A (not B).

Multiple developer initials scattered throughout levels as secret collectibles. Unlock methods vary by level: positional (jumping on grass), binary input (trash can facing direction), timed sequences (door orders), or X-Ray Specs activation. Each yields 1-3 coins upon collection.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Level 2 (Springfield Mall) - Trash can hex-shift register puzzle

notable_level_patterns.description: Skateboard available as equipment but functionally limited to one small section per level; otherwise unused in final build despite presence in prototype.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Level 1 (Streets of Springfield) - Initial traversal section
